prompt: A vendor expanded its catalog feed to include fixed-width identifiers and prefixed identifiers. Imports now report a duplicate for codes such as 0012 and 12 even though the vendor treats them as different items, while a lookup for one prefixed code can return another prefixed item. Existing all-digit codes without leading zeroes still work. Preserve identifier representation and make lookup use the vendor's exact identifier contract.

repo_files.sql/item_lookup.sql:
SELECT id, vendor_code, description
FROM catalog_items
WHERE CAST(vendor_code AS INTEGER) = CAST(:vendor_code AS INTEGER)
ORDER BY id;
repo_files.tests/test_public.py:
import sqlite3
from pathlib import Path


ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
SCHEMA = (ROOT / "sql" / "schema.sql").read_text(encoding="utf-8")
LOOKUP = (ROOT / "sql" / "item_lookup.sql").read_text(encoding="utf-8")


def test_plain_numeric_vendor_code_can_be_imported_and_found():
    database = sqlite3.connect(":memory:")
    database.executescript(SCHEMA)
    database.execute(
        "INSERT INTO catalog_items(id, vendor_code, description) VALUES (?, ?, ?)",
        (1, "8123", "Standard relay"),
    )

    rows = database.execute(LOOKUP, {"vendor_code": "8123"}).fetchall()

    assert len(rows) == 1
    assert rows[0][0] == 1
    assert rows[0][2] == "Standard relay"
